terms.definition: A discrete period during which a system’s key parameters remain relatively stable before undergoing qualitative change
Routine conformity to the authority structures and behavioral expectations of an organization
The process by which a unified institution breaks into autonomous or loosely coupled sub-units
Progressive loss of organizational mass, authority, and participant loyalty
A religious entity meeting at least two of five criteria: minimal governance, digital or multi-site modality, therapeutic/prosperity content, ritual substitution, and institutional displacement
Individuals who self-identify with a faith tradition but seldom or never participate in institutional practices
A method for validating findings by cross-comparing multiple independent data sources measuring related constructs
Systematic variation in survey responses attributable to the method of data collection (e.g., in-person vs. web) rather than the underlying construct
A one-sided mediated interaction in which an audience member feels intimacy with a media persona who is unaware of their existence
A worldview positing a benevolent deity whose main goal is individual happiness and moral niceness
Demographic process whereby younger cohorts with distinct attitudes replace older cohorts, altering aggregate statistics
A belief model in which spiritual practices are viewed as exchanges for material or personal benefits
The phenomenon where engagement with a new entity supplants participation in a former institution
The financial burden created by high, non-variable expenses that hinder organizational agility
